scope: Action, scene, label, and expert-rule maintenance plan after DialogueAct v2 integration.

known_distribution_issue.interpretation: Current synthetic train split is not a balanced six-act policy benchmark. Inform dominates because active-evaluation scenes and reward rules favor demonstration/comparison. Recommend is mostly a pressure-risk contrast, and Greet belongs mainly to transaction/realshoot/terminal data.

required_expert_review_items: Separate Recommend(pressure=soft) from strong recommendation as a positive close-support action.
Revise state_aida_to_candidates rules so recommendation can be valid only under explicit advice/decision-close conditions.
Revise transition rewards for soft vs firm recommendation instead of mapping all recommendation pressure to the old A3 contrast.
Expand Reassure-positive scenarios for hesitation, walkaway, and post-decision reassurance.
Keep Greet mostly in realshoot/target terminal opening and closing clips.
Move all auxiliary acts from top-level co_acts into act_params.supporting_acts on re-export.
must_not_claim: Do not claim PIWM-Train-Synth-v1 is target terminal data.
Do not claim the current 543 rows are balanced across six DialogueActs.
Do not claim expert rules are fully expert-reviewed; coverage currently has zero expert_reviewed rules.
